page: Microsoft Azure E8-2ds_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the EDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz, configured with 1 physical core and 2 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) via hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is provided via a 307 GB temporary disk, and the server is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. No dedicated GPU resources are included.

Benchmark results indicate a divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core processing is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category. However, multi-core and parallelized workloads show limitations, as evidenced by poor ratings in stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU physics tests. Memory performance is mixed; while the Passmark memory score is average, uncached memory read and write bandwidth benchmarks are poor.

The primary tradeoff of this configuration is its high memory capacity relative to its limited compute resources. With only 2 vCPUs, compute-intensive tasks such as software compilation, image processing, and ray tracing register poor benchmark results. Database operations and Redis SET operations also perform in the lower percentiles. Consequently, this server is best suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high parallel processing power, such as small-scale caching layers, lightweight database instances with large working sets, or memory-bound utility applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-2ds_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the EDSv5 family, powered by a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It is configured with 2 vCPUs (1 physical core), 64.0 GB of RAM, and a 307 GB local disk, with support for Premium Storage. The server exhibits a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. Performance benchmarks show average single-core CPU capabilities, but poor multi-core scaling and uncached memory bandwidth. Compute-heavy tasks like software compilation and ray tracing perform poorly, as do database and Redis operations. This server is best suited for memory-bound applications with low parallel processing demands, such as small-scale caching or lightweight databases requiring a large memory footprint relative to compute power.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8-2ds_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 2 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 307 GB local disk, designed for memory-intensive workloads with low parallel compute requirements.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8-2ds_v5 is an x86_64 server with 2 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 307 GB local storage, optimized for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and local storage for memory-bound, low-concurrency workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 2 vCPUs and 1 physical core
64.0 GB of RAM providing a high 32.0 GB per vCPU ratio
307 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average single-core CPU performance but limited multi-core scaling
Best suited for memory-bound workloads with low parallel processing needs
